root_text.an10.90:1.1: Atha kho āyasmā sāriputto yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.an10.90:1.2: “kati nu kho, sāriputta, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno balāni, yehi balehi samannāgato khīṇāsavo bhikkhu āsavānaṁ khayaṁ paṭijānāti:
root_text.an10.90:2.1: “Dasa, bhante, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno balāni, yehi balehi samannāgato khīṇāsavo bhikkhu āsavānaṁ khayaṁ paṭijānāti:
root_text.an10.90:2.4: Idha, bhante, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno aniccato sabbe saṅkhārā yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya sudiṭṭhā honti.
root_text.an10.90:2.5: Yampi, bhante, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno aniccato sabbe saṅkhārā yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya sudiṭṭhā honti, idampi, bhante, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno balaṁ hoti, yaṁ balaṁ āgamma khīṇāsavo bhikkhu āsavānaṁ khayaṁ paṭijānāti:
root_text.an10.90:3.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhante, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno aṅgārakāsūpamā kāmā yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya sudiṭṭhā honti.
root_text.an10.90:3.2: Yampi, bhante, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno aṅgārakāsūpamā kāmā yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya sudiṭṭhā honti, idampi, bhante, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno balaṁ hoti, yaṁ balaṁ āgamma khīṇāsavo bhikkhu āsavānaṁ khayaṁ paṭijānāti:
root_text.an10.90:4.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhante, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno vivekaninnaṁ cittaṁ hoti vivekapoṇaṁ vivekapabbhāraṁ vivekaṭṭhaṁ nekkhammābhirataṁ byantībhūtaṁ sabbaso āsavaṭṭhāniyehi dhammehi.
root_text.an10.90:4.2: Yampi, bhante, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno vivekaninnaṁ cittaṁ hoti vivekapoṇaṁ vivekapabbhāraṁ vivekaṭṭhaṁ nekkhammābhirataṁ byantībhūtaṁ sabbaso āsavaṭṭhāniyehi dhammehi, idampi, bhante, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno balaṁ hoti, yaṁ balaṁ āgamma khīṇāsavo bhikkhu āsavānaṁ khayaṁ paṭijānāti:
root_text.an10.90:5.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhante, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno cattāro satipaṭṭhānā bhāvitā honti subhāvitā.
root_text.an10.90:5.2: Yampi, bhante, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno cattāro satipaṭṭhānā bhāvitā honti subhāvitā, idampi, bhante, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno balaṁ hoti, yaṁ balaṁ āgamma khīṇāsavo bhikkhu āsavānaṁ khayaṁ paṭijānāti:
root_text.an10.90:6.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhante, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno cattāro sammappadhānā bhāvitā honti subhāvitā …pe…
root_text.an10.90:6.6: ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo bhāvito hoti subhāvito.
root_text.an10.90:6.7: Yampi, bhante, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo bhāvito hoti subhāvito, idampi, bhante, khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno balaṁ hoti, yaṁ balaṁ āgamma khīṇāsavo bhikkhu āsavānaṁ khayaṁ paṭijānāti:
root_text.an10.90:7.1: Imāni kho, bhante, dasa khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno balāni, yehi balehi samannāgato khīṇāsavo bhikkhu āsavānaṁ khayaṁ paṭijānāti:

translation_text.an10.90:0.3: The Powers of One Who has Ended Defilements
translation_text.an10.90:1.1: Then Venerable Sāriputta went up to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to him:
translation_text.an10.90:1.2: “Sāriputta, how many powers does a mendicant who has ended the defilements have that qualify them to claim:
translation_text.an10.90:2.1: “Sir, a mendicant who has ended the defilements has ten powers that qualify them to claim:
translation_text.an10.90:2.4: Firstly, a mendicant with defilements ended has clearly seen with right wisdom all conditions as truly impermanent.
translation_text.an10.90:2.5: This is a power that a mendicant who has ended the defilements relies on to claim:
translation_text.an10.90:3.1: Furthermore, a mendicant with defilements ended has clearly seen with right wisdom that sensual pleasures are truly like a pit of glowing coals.
translation_text.an10.90:3.2: This is a power that a mendicant who has ended the defilements relies on to claim:
translation_text.an10.90:4.1: Furthermore, the mind of a mendicant with defilements ended slants, slopes, and inclines to seclusion. They’re withdrawn, loving renunciation, and have totally eliminated defiling influences.
translation_text.an10.90:4.2: This is a power that a mendicant who has ended the defilements relies on to claim:
translation_text.an10.90:5.1: Furthermore, a mendicant with defilements ended has well developed the four kinds of mindfulness meditation.
translation_text.an10.90:5.2: This is a power that a mendicant who has ended the defilements relies on to claim:
translation_text.an10.90:6.1: Furthermore, a mendicant with defilements ended has well developed the four right efforts. …
translation_text.an10.90:6.2: the four bases of psychic power …
translation_text.an10.90:6.7: This is a power that a mendicant who has ended the defilements relies on to claim:
translation_text.an10.90:7.1: A mendicant who has ended the defilements possesses these ten powers that qualify them to claim:
